tool.name: create_session
tool.description:
Create a new session for authenticated testing.
Creates and stores a session with cookies, headers, and authentication tokens for use across multiple requests during penetration testing.
This is useful when testing authenticated endpoints or maintaining state across multiple tool invocations.
Supported authentication types: - bearer: Bearer token in Authorization header (default) - basic: HTTP Basic authentication - api_key: API key in X-API-Key header - custom: Custom authentication (token stored for manual use)

parameters.parameter: Unique name for this session (e.g., "admin_session", "user_auth")
Base URL for the session (e.g., "https://target.example.com")
Initial cookies as a dict (e.g., {"session_id": "abc123", "lang": "en"})
Custom headers as a dict (e.g., {"X-Custom-Header": "value"})
Authentication token (JWT, API key, or credentials)
Token type: "bearer" (default), "basic", "api_key", "custom"

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether session was created - session_id: Unique ID to reference this session - session_name: The name provided - base_url: Base URL if provided - cookie_count: Number of cookies stored - header_count: Number of custom headers - auth_type: Authentication type if token provided - message: Status message
tool.example:
create_session(session_name="main_session", base_url="https://target.com")
create_session( session_name="admin_auth", base_url="https://api.target.com", auth_token="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...", auth_type="bearer" )
create_session( session_name="logged_in", cookies={"PHPSESSID": "abc123", "user_id": "42"} )

tool.name: update_session
tool.description:
Update an existing session with new cookies, headers, or CSRF token.
Use this to add cookies received from Set-Cookie headers, update authorization tokens, or store CSRF tokens extracted from responses.

parameters.parameter: Session ID to update (from create_session result)
Cookies to add or update (e.g., {"new_cookie": "value"})
Headers to add or update
CSRF token to store for this session
If true (default), merge with existing data. If false, replace entirely.

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether update succeeded - session_id: The session ID - session_name: Session name - cookie_count: Total cookies after update - header_count: Total headers after update - has_csrf: Whether session has a CSRF token - message: Status message

tool.name: get_session_info
tool.description:
Get information about a specific session or list all active sessions.
Use this to inspect session state, verify cookies are set correctly, or get a list of all sessions for cleanup.
NOTE: Sensitive values (tokens, session cookies) are redacted in output.

parameters.parameter: Specific session ID to query
If true, list all active sessions (ignores session_id)

tool.returns:
JSON object containing (for single session): - success: Whether query succeeded - session_id: The session ID - name: Session name - base_url: Base URL - cookies: Cookies (values redacted) - headers: Headers (auth values redacted) - csrf_token: CSRF token (redacted) - auth: Authentication info - created_at/updated_at: Timestamps
For list_all=true: - success: Whether query succeeded - sessions: List of session summaries - total_count: Number of active sessions

tool.name: extract_csrf_token
tool.description:
Extract CSRF token from HTML content.
Searches for common CSRF token patterns in HTML forms, meta tags, and JavaScript code. Can automatically store the token in a session.
Detected patterns: - Hidden input fields: csrf_token, _csrf, csrfmiddlewaretoken, _token, authenticity_token, __RequestVerificationToken - Meta tags: <meta name="csrf-token" content="..."> - JavaScript: csrfToken = "...", window.__csrf = "..." - Data attributes: data-csrf="..."

parameters.parameter: HTML or JavaScript content to search for CSRF tokens
Specific token name to look for (e.g., "my_csrf_field")
Session ID to store the extracted token in

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether any tokens were found - tokens: List of found tokens with metadata - token_count: Number of tokens found - primary_token: The first (most likely) token value - stored_in_session: Session ID if token was stored - message: Status message
tool.example:
extract_csrf_token(content="<html>...<input name='csrf_token' value='abc123'>...</html>")
extract_csrf_token( content=response_body, session_id="abc12345" )
extract_csrf_token( content=response_body, token_name="my_app_token" )
tool.workflow: 1. Fetch a page that contains a form (e.g., login page) 2. Use extract_csrf_token() on the response body 3. Include the token in subsequent POST requests 4. Optionally store in session with session_id parameter

tool.name: manage_cookies
tool.description:
Parse, analyze, and manage HTTP cookies.
Actions: - parse: Parse Set-Cookie header or cookie string into dict - analyze: Analyze cookie security flags (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite) - export: Export session cookies as Cookie header string
Use this to: - Parse cookies from HTTP responses - Check for security misconfigurations in session cookies - Format cookies for manual HTTP requests

parameters.parameter: Action to perform: "parse", "analyze", "export"
Cookies as string or dict (for parse action)
Session ID (required for export action)
Raw Set-Cookie or Cookie header string

tool.returns:
For parse: - success: Whether parsing succeeded - cookies: Parsed cookie dict - cookie_count: Number of cookies
For analyze: - success: Whether analysis succeeded - analysis: Security analysis results including: - secure_flags: Which flags are present - findings: Security issues found - security_score: 0-100 score - is_session_cookie: Whether it appears to be a session cookie - recommendation: Suggested action
For export: - success: Whether export succeeded - cookie_header: Formatted Cookie header string - cookie_count: Number of cookies exported
tool.example:
manage_cookies( action="parse", cookie_header="session=abc123; Path=/; HttpOnly; Secure" )
manage_cookies( action="analyze", cookie_header="session=abc123; Path=/" )
manage_cookies( action="export", session_id="abc12345" )
tool.workflow: 1. Receive Set-Cookie from server response 2. Parse: manage_cookies(action="parse", cookie_header="...") 3. Analyze: manage_cookies(action="analyze", cookie_header="...") 4. If security issues found, report in vulnerability assessment 5. Store cookies in session: update_session(session_id="...", cookies={...})
